function.description: Browse and list all files/directories in a GitHub repository. Efficiently retrieves the complete repository structure in a single API call when used with recursive mode. Perfect for analyzing codebase structure, finding specific files, or getting an overview of repository contents without cloning.
function.name: GITHUB_GET_A_TREE

parameters.description: Request schema for `GetATree`

owner.description: Repository owner username or organization name (e.g., for github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, owner is 'kubernetes').
owner.title: Owner

recursive.description: Set to True (or '1'/'true') to fetch the ENTIRE repository file tree recursively (all files and directories in one call). Set to False or leave empty to fetch only the top-level files/folders. For browsing or analyzing the full repository structure, use recursive=True.
recursive.title: Recursive

repo.description: Repository name without owner or .git extension (e.g., for github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, repo is 'kubernetes').
repo.title: Repo

tree_sha.description: Branch name (e.g., 'main', 'master'), tag name (e.g., 'v1.0.0'), commit SHA, or tree SHA. Directory paths are NOT supported - use a branch/tag to get the full tree, then extract the directory's SHA from results.
tree_sha.title: Tree Sha

parameters.title: GetATreeRequest

username.title: Username

parameters.description: Defines the parameters for retrieving a specific GitHub Actions workflow from a repository.

owner.description: Username or organization name owning the repository (case-insensitive).
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).
repo.title: Repo

workflow_id.description: Unique numeric identifier of the workflow; use this or `workflow_name`.
workflow_id.title: Workflow Id

workflow_name.description: Full name of the workflow file (e.g., `ci.yml`); use this or `workflow_id`.
workflow_name.title: Workflow Name

parameters.title: GetWorkflowRequest

function.description: Gets a specific workflow run by its ID from a GitHub repository.
function.name: GITHUB_GET_A_WORKFLOW_RUN

parameters.description: Request schema for `GetAWorkflowRun`

exclude_pull_requests.description: If true, excludes pull request data; the `pull_requests` array in the response will be empty.
exclude_pull_requests.title: Exclude Pull Requests

owner.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository (not case-sensitive).
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: Name of the repository (case-insensitive, without .git extension).
repo.title: Repo

run_id.description: Unique ID of the workflow run.
run_id.title: Run Id

parameters.title: GetAWorkflowRunRequest

function.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific attempt of a workflow run in a GitHub repository, including its status, conclusion, and timings.
function.name: GITHUB_GET_A_WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT

parameters.description: Request schema for `GetAWorkflowRunAttempt`.

attempt_number.description: The attempt number of the specific workflow run. Each re-run of a workflow creates a new attempt number, starting from 1.
attempt_number.title: Attempt Number

exclude_pull_requests.description: If `true`, excludes pull request data from the API response; the `pull_requests` array in the response will then be empty.
exclude_pull_requests.title: Exclude Pull Requests

owner.description: The account owner of the repository, such as a GitHub username or organization name. This name is not case sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

run_id.description: The unique identifier (ID) of the workflow run. This ID is specific to a repository and can be obtained from API calls that list workflow runs.
run_id.title: Run Id

parameters.title: GetAWorkflowRunAttemptRequest

function.description: Retrieves the billing usage report for a specified GitHub user. Use when you need to analyze billing costs and usage patterns.
function.name: GITHUB_GET_BILLING_USAGE_REPORT_USER

parameters.description: Request to get billing usage report for a GitHub user.

day.description: If specified, only return results for a single day. The value of `day` is an integer between `1` and `31`. If no `year` or `month` is specified, the default `year` and `month` are used.
day.title: Day

month.description: If specified, only return results for a single month. The value of `month` is an integer between `1` and `12`. If no year is specified the default `year` is used.
month.title: Month

username.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.
username.title: Username

year.description: If specified, only return results for a single year. The value of `year` is an integer with four digits representing a year. For example, `2025`. Default value is the current year.
year.title: Year

parameters.title: GetBillingUsageReportUserRequest

function.description: Fetches commit authors identified during a repository import, used to map authors from an external VCS to GitHub accounts. NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

since.description: An optional author ID. If provided, only authors with an ID greater than this specified ID will be returned. This ID refers to the `id` field of an author object in the response, and can be used for paginating through the list of authors.
since.title: Since

parameters.title: GetCommitAuthorsRequest

parameters.title: GetDefaultWorkflowPermissionsForARepositoryRequest

function.description: Gets the dependency diff between two Git revisions in a repository, where 'basehead' specifies the revisions and 'name' can optionally scope to a specific manifest file.
function.name: GITHUB_GET_DEPENDENCY_DIFF

parameters.description: Request schema for `GetDependencyDiff`

basehead.description: A string specifying the base and head Git revisions to compare, in the format `BASE_REF...HEAD_REF`. `BASE_REF` and `HEAD_REF` can be commit SHAs, branch names, or tags. For example, `main...develop` or `v1.0.0...v1.1.0`.
basehead.title: Basehead

name.description: The optional full path, relative to the repository root, of a specific dependency manifest file (e.g., `package-lock.json`, `pom.xml`). If provided, the diff will be scoped to this file.
name.title: Name

owner.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: GetDependencyDiffRequest

function.description: Gets a specific deploy key, identified by its `key_id`, for the GitHub repository specified by `owner` and `repo`.
function.name: GITHUB_GET_DEPLOY_KEY

parameters.description: Request schema to get a specific deploy key for a repository.

key_id.description: The unique numerical identifier of the deploy key.
key_id.title: Key Id

owner.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: GetDeployKeyRequest

function.description: Gets a specific deployment by ID from a repository, provided the repository and deployment ID exist.
function.name: GITHUB_GET_DEPLOYMENT

parameters.description: Request schema for retrieving a specific deployment within a GitHub repository.

deployment_id.description: The unique numeric identifier of the deployment to retrieve.
deployment_id.title: Deployment Id

owner.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: GetDeploymentRequest

function.description: Retrieves a specific deployment branch policy for an environment in a repository, identified by its unique ID.
function.name: GITHUB_GET_DEPLOYMENT_BRANCH_POLICY

parameters.description: Request schema for retrieving a specific deployment branch policy for an environment.

branch_policy_id.description: The unique identifier of the branch policy.
branch_policy_id.title: Branch Policy Id

environment_name.description: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded; for example, replace any slashes in the name with `%2F`.
environment_name.title: Environment Name

owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: GetDeploymentBranchPolicyRequest
